edges.evidence_quote: According to 2006 research reports, "Polaco" (the chief of the sub-group Los Polacos and one of the leaders of Los Diablos Rojos) was the bodyguard of Hugo Moyano (the secretary general of the CGT) 15 years ago and still holds that role.

LRGP.description: LRGP (Lombard, Rother, Gibson, and Playter) was a gang based in the Broadway-Fillmore area of Buffalo, New York, founded in the 2000s. It was primarily involved in drug trafficking, including marijuana and crack cocaine, and engaged in violent conflict with the rival Bailey Boys. The gang was heavily targeted by law enforcement, leading to multiple arrests and its eventual disbandment by 2016.

descriptions: Bailey Boys are rivals with LRGP.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: LRGP , Milla Gangsta Bloods

descriptions: LRGP is the direct opposition to the Bailey Boys, indicating a rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Lombard, Rother, Gibson, and Playter ( LRGP ), also known as the LRGP Crew , is/was a gang based in Buffalo, New York in the Broadway-Fillmore area of the city and is the direct opposition to the Bailey Boys .

descriptions: The infobox lists Bloods as allies of LRGP.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bloods

descriptions: Dhak-Duhre coalition has strong ties with the United Nations gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dhak-Duhre coalition is known to have developed strong ties with the UN

descriptions: Dhak-Duhre coalition waged war against Wolf Pack.
edges.evidence_quote: waged a war against the Wolf Pack

Benson Syndicate.description: The Benson Syndicate was a 19th-century criminal organization in the western United States that fraudulently executed or fabricated cadastral land surveys for the U.S. General Land Office. Led by John A. Benson, it operated from the mid-1870s to 1898, primarily in California with headquarters in San Francisco. The syndicate infiltrated government positions, including California Surveyors General, to award contracts and approve fraudulent surveys, affecting nearly 20 million acres. Its downfall began in 1885 when ground inspections were instituted.

Mau Maus.description: A 1950s Puerto Rican street gang based in Brooklyn, New York City, formed in 1957 by youths from the Fort Greene Housing Project. Known for its violent activities including murder and drug trafficking, the gang gained notoriety through its leader Nicky Cruz, whose story was documented in the book and film 'The Cross and the Switchblade'. The gang was active until around 1962.

descriptions: The Mau Maus are listed as a rival of the Puerto Rican Mob in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: La Rompe ONU , The Corporation , Paulino Castillo Organization (conflict with some families) , Mau Maus (defunct) , Medellín Cartel (defunct) , Gulf Cartel , Grupo 27

descriptions: The Mau Maus were formed by members who broke away from the Apaches.
edges.evidence_quote: Some members of a street gang called the Apaches broke away and created the Mau Maus

descriptions: The Mau Maus retaliated against the Bishops for killing one of their members.
edges.evidence_quote: Around January 1958 a member of the rival Bishop gang and a candy store owner were stabbed to death by some of the Mau Mau gang, supposedly in retaliation for the Bishops' killing of Mannie Durango

descriptions: The Mau Maus sought and received permission from the Chaplains to form, and initially used the name Mau Mau Chaplains.
edges.evidence_quote: They also asked permission from a rival gang called the Chaplains to start a Puerto Rican gang in the area. Eventually the gang was called Mau Mau Chaplains.

descriptions: The Mau Maus killed a member of the Sand Street Angels, an enemy gang.
edges.evidence_quote: shot and killed Anthony Lavonchino, a member of the Sand Street Angels, one of the Mau Maus' enemies

descriptions: A former Mau Maus member founded and led the Vampires.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvador Agrón, a member of the Mau Maus, later led another gang called the Vampires

descriptions: The Mau Maus were bitter enemies of South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Also bitter enemies of SBB were the Puerto Rican Gangs, The Untouchable Bishops, and the Apaches, and the African American gangs The Mau Maus, and the Chaplains.

descriptions: The Zagaria faction is a faction within the Casalesi.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos expanded into Germany in June 2000 when the club "patched over" seventeen chapters of the Ghostrider's, Road Eagles and Destroyers that had been serving as probationary chapters since the previous year.
